channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: Supercharge your AI agents: The New ADK Integrations Ecosystem
item.description: The Agent Development Kit (ADK) now features a wide range of third-party tool integrations, enhancing the capabilities of AI agents for real-world applications.

item.title: How we built the Google I/O 2026 Save the Date experience
item.description: Google I/O 2026 is set for May 19-20, featuring a save the date puzzle that showcases AI's potential to enhance event experiences.

item.title: You can't stream the energy: A developer's guide to Google Cloud Next '26 in Vegas
item.description: Google Cloud Next '26 in Las Vegas will focus on networking and hands-on learning, emphasizing the shift to agentic AI with various technical tracks.

item.title: What's new in TensorFlow 2.21
item.description: Google has launched LiteRT, a new framework that enhances GPU and NPU acceleration, supporting PyTorch and JAX, and aims to improve on-device inference for GenAI.

item.title: Introducing Wednesday Build Hour
item.description: The Wednesday Build Hour is a new interactive session by Google Cloud aimed at helping developers enhance their cloud skills through hands-on activities.

item.title: Unleash Your Development Superpowers: Refining the Core Coding Experience
item.description: Gemini Code Assist has introduced updates to improve coding workflows, including features like Agent Mode and Inline Diff Views for better collaboration.

item.title: Introducing Finish Changes and Outlines, now available in Gemini Code Assist extensions on IntelliJ and VS Code
item.description: New features in Gemini Code Assist for IntelliJ and VS Code, such as Finish Changes and Outlines, aim to streamline coding and enhance comprehension.

item.title: The US Army Is Building Its Own Chatbot for Combat
item.description: The US Army is developing a chatbot designed to provide soldiers with mission-critical information based on real military data.

item.title: RAF eyes cheap drone-killer as Typhoon jet tests laser-guided rockets
item.description: BAE Systems has tested a laser-guided rocket system with the RAF's Typhoon jet, aiming to develop a cost-effective solution against drone threats.

item.title: Android users can get up to $100 each from this class action suit - see if you're eligible
item.description: A class action suit against Google claims unauthorized data transmission over cellular connections, potentially allowing Android users to claim up to $100 each.

item.title: E-books are more expensive now, too - 5 ways I find good free Kindle reads in 2026
item.description: The article shares tips on finding free Kindle e-books, highlighting the growing costs of e-books in 2026. It aims to help readers expand their digital libraries without spending money.

item.title: Amazon is ending support for 8 Kindle models next month: Is yours on the list?
item.description: Amazon will stop supporting eight older Kindle models next month, preventing access to the Kindle Store. Users are advised to consider alternatives for reading.

item.title: 5 Burning Questions About Elon Musk’s Terafab Chip Partnership with Intel
item.description: Elon Musk's partnership with Intel for the Terafab chip project raises questions about its feasibility and the specifics of Intel's involvement. The collaboration's success remains uncertain.

item.title: Home Depot's Spring Black Friday sale starts this week: Here's what to know
item.description: Home Depot is launching its Spring Black Friday sale, offering significant discounts on various outdoor products. Shoppers are advised to prepare for the sale.

item.title: Anthropic’s New Product Aims to Handle the Hard Part of Building AI Agents
item.description: Anthropic is introducing a new product designed to simplify the development of AI agents, aiming to make it easier for businesses to adopt AI technology.

item.title: Minnesota State payroll problems grew after Workday launch, auditors say
item.description: Minnesota State's transition to a new Workday HR platform has resulted in payroll issues, affecting many faculty and staff. Auditors have reported significant errors and delays.

item.title: Code Security risk assessment available for organizations
item.description: A new free Code Security risk assessment tool is now available for organizations to identify and summarize security vulnerabilities, helping improve overall security posture.

item.title: After two years of testing, wind power still can't replace solar for me - here's why
item.description: After testing wind power for two years, the author concludes that solar energy is more efficient and easier to maintain than portable wind turbines.

item.title: The 10 most popular products ZDNET readers bought last month (including a tiny storage option)
item.description: ZDNET reveals the top ten products purchased by readers last month, including various tech gadgets and accessories, reflecting current consumer trends.

item.title: How a burner email can protect your inbox - setting one up one is easy and free
item.description: The article explains how using a burner email can protect your primary inbox from spam and security risks, providing a simple method for setting one up.

item.title: GitHub Universe is back: We want you to take the stage
item.description: GitHub Universe is returning, inviting participants to share memorable sessions and inspire others in the tech community.

item.title: Talk ain't cheap: DARPA offers grants for new AI-to-AI communication protocol
item.description: DARPA is funding research to develop a new AI-to-AI communication protocol to enhance collaboration among AI agents for scientific discovery.

item.title: How Amazon uses agentic AI for vulnerability detection at global scale
item.description: Amazon's RuleForge system utilizes agentic AI to create detection rules for vulnerabilities 336% faster than traditional methods.

item.title: New PGP signing key for GitHub CLI Linux packages
item.description: GitHub has released a new PGP signing key for its CLI Linux packages, ensuring secure package management.

item.title: Astropad’s Workbench reimagines remote desktop for AI agents, not IT support
item.description: Astropad's Workbench allows users to remotely control AI agents on Mac Minis via iPhone or iPad, offering low-latency access.

item.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
item.description: During National Robotics Week, NVIDIA showcases advancements in physical AI, highlighting robots' transformative impact across various industries.

item.title: OpenAI releases a new safety blueprint to address the rise in child sexual exploitation
item.description: OpenAI has introduced a Child Safety Blueprint to combat the rise in child sexual exploitation associated with AI advancements.

item.title: Databricks co-founder wins prestigious ACM award, says ‘AGI is here already’
item.description: Databricks co-founder Matei Zaharia received a prestigious ACM award and claims that AGI is already present but misunderstood.

item.title: Microsoft calls time on ASP.NET Core 2.3 on .NET Framework
item.description: Microsoft has announced the end-of-support date for ASP.NET Core 2.3, marking a transition in its development tools.

item.title: ALTK‑Evolve: On‑the‑Job Learning for AI Agents
item.description: ALTK-Evolve focuses on on-the-job learning for AI agents, enhancing their adaptability and performance in real-world tasks.

item.title: Safetensors is Joining the PyTorch Foundation
item.description: Safetensors is now part of the PyTorch Foundation, enhancing its commitment to safe and efficient tensor storage. This move is significant for developers focusing on machine learning and AI safety.

item.title: Manage AI costs with Amazon Bedrock Projects
item.description: Amazon Bedrock Projects allows users to track AI inference costs by specific workloads, facilitating better cost management in AWS. This feature is crucial for organizations looking to optimize their AI spending.

item.title: Outlook: Copilot Search Suggestions for Outlook
item.description: Outlook will introduce Copilot suggestions in its search menu to enhance user productivity by streamlining inbox and calendar management. This feature is set to launch in June 2026.

item.title: Microsoft Viva: New Overview experience and agent categories in Agent Dashboard
item.description: Microsoft Viva's new Overview page in the Agent Dashboard will provide insights on agent usage and adoption within organizations. This feature aims to enhance visibility for businesses starting in June 2026.

item.title: Microsoft Viva: Unlocking Deeper Copilot Insights with Enhanced Power BI Filtering
item.description: Enhanced filtering options in Power BI reports for Microsoft Viva will allow admins to tailor insights to their business needs. This feature is expected to be available by May 2026.

item.title: Outlook: Data Loss Prevention (DLP) Policy Tips & Override in Outlook for Mac
item.description: Outlook for Mac introduces Data Loss Prevention (DLP) policy tips to help users avoid accidental data leaks. This feature will be available in May 2026, enhancing data security.

item.title: 80 Copilots later, Microsoft is finally confronting the mess
item.description: Microsoft acknowledges the overwhelming presence of its Copilot AI in Windows 11 and plans to simplify its integration. This recognition comes after user feedback highlighted the clutter caused by excessive AI features.

item.title: Building AI defenses at scale: Before the threats emerge
item.description: AWS emphasizes its commitment to building scalable AI defenses to protect customers from emerging threats. This proactive approach highlights the importance of security in AI deployments.

item.title: Third-Party Agents Lose Access as Anthropic Tightens Claude Usage Rules
item.description: Anthropic has tightened usage rules for its Claude AI, resulting in third-party agents losing access. This change reflects growing concerns over AI resource consumption and management.

item.title: College Students Losing Ability to Participate in Class Discussions Since They Offloaded Their Thinking to AI
item.description: A study reveals that college students are struggling to engage in class discussions as they increasingly rely on AI for thinking tasks. This trend raises concerns about critical thinking skills.

item.title: ✍️ how to rank for AEO, prove marketing ROI, and market to nerds
item.description: The article discusses strategies for ranking in AEO, demonstrating marketing ROI, and effectively targeting niche audiences, particularly 'nerds'. This guidance is significant for marketers looking to optimize their outreach.

item.title: Anthropic touts AI cybersecurity project with Big Tech partners
item.description: Anthropic has launched a cybersecurity initiative called Project Glasswing, collaborating with major tech firms like Amazon, Microsoft, and Apple to preview an advanced AI model for cybersecurity.

item.title: Apple, Google, and Microsoft join Anthropic's Project Glasswing to defend world's most critical software
item.description: Twelve major tech companies, including Apple and Google, are collaborating on Anthropic's Project Glasswing, utilizing the unreleased Mythos model to identify software vulnerabilities proactively.

item.title: Rising to the Era of AI-powered Cyber Defense
item.description: Cisco has joined Anthropic’s Project Glasswing, committing to defend against AI-powered cyber threats alongside other major cybersecurity players.

item.title: XRP 1-Year MVRV Falls To -41%, Lowest Since FTX Crash
item.description: XRP's one-year market value to realized value (MVRV) ratio has dropped to -41%, the lowest since the FTX crash, indicating potential investment opportunities.

item.title: Anthropic limits Mythos AI rollout over fears hackers could use model for cyberattacks
item.description: Anthropic is limiting the rollout of its Mythos AI model due to concerns that it could be exploited for cyberattacks, despite its use in Project Glasswing.

item.title: Your Migration’s Source of Truth: The Modernization Assessment
item.description: The article introduces GitHub Copilot Modernization and discusses its assessment report as part of the cloud modernization process, aiming to improve migration strategies.

item.title: I tried using ChatGPT in CarPlay — and it was weirder than I expected
item.description: The author shares their experience using ChatGPT in CarPlay, noting that it transforms mundane drives into more engaging and conversational journeys.

item.title: Fire Emblem: Three Houses Characters Become Kids in Fire Emblem Heroes
item.description: Fire Emblem Heroes has launched a new banner featuring characters from Fire Emblem: Three Houses as children, adding a nostalgic twist to the game.

item.title: iPhone Fold Expected to Launch on Time in September Despite Delay Rumors
item.description: Reports indicate that Apple's iPhone Fold is on track for a September launch, despite previous rumors of delays, ensuring it aligns with the typical iPhone release schedule.
